ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific code exists for a combined sachet-and-candy gift set. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers instant coffee powder; the candy component is not separately coded. Composite product approximation used for the sachet component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a composite instant coffee sachet and candy retail set.

preparation_method: instant preparation (dissolve sachet in hot water)
allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain milk proteins)
soy (non-dairy creamer may contain soy lecithin)

serving.description: 1 box of kopi sachets and 1 packet of kopi candy
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full retail set comprising one box of instant kopi sachets and one packet of kopi candy, totalling approximately 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo Database); supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar (FDC ID 14215) for composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board, Singapore; United States Department of Agriculture
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB or USDA entry exists for a combined kopi-sachet-and-candy retail set. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo data for sweetened instant coffee (3-in-1 type) and USDA FDC ID 14215 (instant coffee with whitener and sugar), blended to reflect the composite product. Input values (energy 225 kcal, protein 2.5 g, fat 7.5 g, carbohydrate 37.5 g, sugar 30.0 g, sodium 75 mg, dietary fibre 1.0 g, saturated fat 6.0 g, trans fat 0.05 g, cholesterol 0.0 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this source range and are retained. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. — Analogous food: instant coffee with sugar and whitener (3-in-1 type), GI ~79 (glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for a combined kopi-sachet-and-candy retail set. GI of 79 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sweetened instant coffee with non-dairy creamer (3-in-1 type), which is dominated by rapidly absorbed sugars (30 g/100 g) and refined carbohydrates from the candy component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (79 × 75.0 g) / 100 = 59.3. Available carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 37.5 × 2 = 75.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (30 g/100 g) in a sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; saturated fat (6 g/100 g) from palm-based non-dairy creamer contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this product entirely and substitute with plain kopi-o kosong (unsweetened black coffee) to eliminate the 30 g/100 g sugar load and 6 g/100 g saturated fat that independently raise cardiovascular and blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines on sugar-sweetened beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 30 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; GI ~79 (high); glycemic load per 200 g serving is approximately 59.3 — extremely high; only 1 g dietary fibre per 100 g provides no meaningful glucose buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes must avoid this product: the 30 g/100 g sugar content, high GI (~79), and negligible fibre (1 g/100 g) cause rapid and severe postprandial glucose spikes, and the palm-based non-dairy creamer worsens insulin resistance; plain black coffee (kopi-o kosong) with no added sugar is the recommended substitute.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 6 g/100 g from palm-based non-dairy creamer raises LDL cholesterol; trans fat 0.05 g/100 g present; sugar 30 g/100 g promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial dysfunction
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product due to the combination of 6 g/100 g saturated fat (palm-based creamer), 0.05 g/100 g trans fat, and 30 g/100 g added sugar, all of which raise LDL cholesterol, promote hypertriglyceridaemia, and impair endothelial function; switching to plain black coffee eliminates all three risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: 30 g/100 g sugar and 6 g/100 g saturated fat from palm-based non-dairy creamer are strongly pro-inflammatory; no anti-inflammatory offset present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as the 30 g/100 g sugar load promotes inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6) and the 6 g/100 g palm-based saturated fat negates any anti-inflammatory benefit from coffee polyphenols; plain black coffee is the recommended substitute.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi Duo is a Singaporean retail coffee gift set comprising one box of instant kopi sachets (choice of flavour, typically 3-in-1 style with sugar and non-dairy creamer) and one packet of kopi candy. The sachet component is prepared by dissolving the powder in hot water; the candy is ready-to-eat. Per 100 g of the combined product, it provides approximately 225 kcal, 2.5 g protein, 7.5 g fat (of which 6.0 g is saturated fat from palm-based non-dairy creamer and 0.05 g is trans fat), 37.5 g carbohydrate, and 30.0 g sugar — a very high sugar load. Dietary fibre is only 1.0 g/100 g and sodium is 75 mg/100 g. The product is ultra-processed and classified as a sugar-sweetened instant coffee product. It is halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable but not vegan due to potential milk derivatives in the non-dairy creamer. Clinically, this product is high-concern for Type 2 Diabetes (GI ~79, GL ~59 per 200 g serving, sugar 30 g/100 g), hypertension (sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar together raise LDL and promote hypertriglyceridaemia). The kopi candy component contributes additional refined sugar and glucose syrup. This product is commonly sold as a gift set or souvenir item in Singapore and is associated with local coffee culture (kopitiam tradition). Plain black kopi-o kosong is the clinically preferred alternative for all three high-concern conditions.
common_user_queries: Is Kopi Duo suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink Kopi Duo if I have high blood pressure?
What is in a kopi sachet set?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB or USDA entry exists for this specific composite retail set (sachet + candy). Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo and USDA FDC ID 14215 (instant coffee with whitener and sugar), which are consistent with the input values. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (3-in-1 sweetened instant coffee). Confidence is medium due to composite product estimation and absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite instant coffee and candy set is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite retail product are not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FDC for this product category. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 14215 for instant coffee with non-dairy creamer — plant-based creamer contains no cholesterol). trans_fat_g is 0.05 g/100 g as reported in input; retained as a low but non-zero value consistent with palm-based non-dairy creamer formulations. GI of 79 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 for sweetened instant coffee with whitener (3-in-1 type). Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (79 × 75.0) / 100 = 59.3. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was noted but the canonical HPB Foodo URL (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) is used as the approved source reference. The cultural primary_cuisine field in the input was listed as 'Toast' which appears to be a data entry error; corrected to 'Singaporean' based on product origin and context.
